task.domains: (topic task) (topic hi-d) (topic ui-d) (topic sw-d) (topic indexing-d) (topic ut-d) a(base ishcondition) a(base varid) a(base varref) a(base cid) a(base partslist) (topic oce-d) (topic sdlVariables-c) (topic oceCommonElements-c) (topic oceMetaDecl-c) (topic oceTopic-c) (topic oceTopic-c task oceTask-c) (topic oceTopic-c hi-d oceHighlight-c) (topic oceTopic-c ui-d oceUi-c) (topic oceTopic-c sw-d oceSoftware-c)
task.title: How to diagnose a license retrieval error when requesting the license from ORS

section.title: 5.1 Error when making the license request

entry.p: The machine was previously installed in a showroom and was equipped with a NFR license
The dongle can not contain NFR licenses in combination with customer licenses.
Replace the dongle and re-execute the license retrieval process.

section.title: 5.2 Error when making the license request
section.p: When requesting the license, the following error may occur:

li.p: The connection cannot be established when activating the license request
Possible causes :
Remote connection not enabled
Proxy settings not correctly set
Connection error at back-office between DRS and ILMS servers
Product license not filled
Actions
Check Remote connection setting
Check Proxy setting
See section 1 Enable Remote connection on the controller
Fill out the product license.
Check with back-office

section.title: 5.3 Error when installing the license
section.p: When installing the license via ORS (" Install license from Remote Server") the following error occurs:

li.p: Possible causes :
The license has not been generated yet and so not yet retrieved by the controller (it takes normally less than 5-6 minutes)
The license has not been generated at ILMS due to:
a wrong product license
a wrong license request (e.g. there was nothing new in the license request compared to the existing current license)
The network connection with the back-office through ORS is broken
Actions :
Check “Transaction log” at back-office to see the license message (This require access to http://service.cpp.canon):
Connect to https://service.cpp.canon
Select your matching account type and login
Select the appropriate printer with its serial number
Go to “Remote data” / “Transaction Log” and check for License activation message
Example of a successful license generation:
The DEV_REQ_LICENSEUPDATE_CREATED indicates that the license is waiting in the back-office to be picked by the machine.
The DEV_REQ_LICENSEUPDATE_RESPOND indicates that the license has arrived at the machine.
Example of a license generation failure (Product license was not correct and unknown in the ILMS database):
The DEV_REQ_CONSOLEMSG_CREATED indicates that the product license that was used in the request does not exist.
Wait 5 minutes and retry “Install license from Remote Server” feature (Settings Editor).
Check whether the Product license is correct (See “Preparation section at the beginning of this document).
Check whether the license required has something new compared to the current license.
Check with the back-office.
